news.headline: Craig-Hallum Remains a Buy on Delcath Systems (DCTH)
news.summary: Craig-Hallum reaffirmed its Buy rating on DCTH, keeping a price target well above the current share price. The note reflects continued sell-side confidence in the HEPZATO commercial ramp and pipeline expansion.

news.headline: Delcath Systems Announces First Patient Dosed in Phase 2 Clinical Trial of HEPZATO in Liver-Dominant Metastatic Breast Cancer
news.summary: The first patient was dosed at the European Institute of Oncology in Milan in the randomized Phase 2 DELUMA trial (NCT06875128, ~90 patients across 20+ US/EU sites) evaluating HEPZATO plus standard of care in HER2-negative liver-dominant metastatic breast cancer. This is Delcath's key pipeline expansion beyond uveal melanoma into a much larger indication.

news.headline: Delcath Systems (NASDAQ:DCTH) Stock Rating Upgraded by Zacks Research
news.summary: Zacks Research upgraded DCTH from a "strong sell" rating to a "hold" rating, part of a broader mix of analyst views (average rating "Moderate Buy") as the stock trades near its 52-week highs.

news.headline: Delcath Systems Added to Russell 2000 Value, 2500 Value, 3000 Value and Microcap Value Benchmarks
news.summary: DCTH was included in several Russell value benchmarks in the June 2026 semi-annual reconstitution, which took effect after market close on June 26. Index membership broadens passive-fund ownership, visibility and liquidity for the small-cap name.

news.headline: Delcath Systems Announces Two ASCO 2026 Investigator Initiated Trials-in-Progress Abstracts
news.summary: Two investigator-initiated trials-in-progress abstracts featuring HEPZATO were published ahead of the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting in Chicago, expanding clinical evidence and physician engagement. This reinforces the growing academic base supporting HEPZATO across indications.

news.headline: Delcath Systems Reports First Quarter 2026 Results and Business Highlights
news.summary: Q1 2026 revenue rose to $25.0M (from $19.8M a year earlier) with HEPZATO KIT contributing the bulk at roughly 85% gross margin and about 36% HEPZATO volume growth; the company held $89.3M cash with no debt and reiterated at least $100M full-year 2026 revenue guidance.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings
catalysts.detail: Second-quarter results are expected in early August (Q2 2025 was reported August 6, and the specific 2026 date had not yet been announced as of mid-July), a key test of the $100M+ full-year revenue guidance and HEPZATO KIT volume growth after a strong Q1.

catalysts.label: $100M+ revenue target
catalysts.detail: Full-year 2026 guidance of at least $100M revenue implies substantial HEPZATO KIT volume growth over 2025; execution against this is the primary near-term driver.

catalysts.label: DELUMA breast cancer trial enrollment
catalysts.detail: Phase 2 HEPZATO metastatic breast cancer trial (NCT06875128, ~90 patients, 20+ US/EU sites) is now dosing patients after the first patient was treated in Milan on July 9; further site activation and enrollment updates are expected.

catalysts.label: mCRC Phase 2 interim data / indication expansion
catalysts.detail: The global Phase 2 trial of HEPZATO plus trifluridine-tipiracil and bevacizumab in liver-dominant metastatic colorectal cancer (first patient dosed August 2025) is ongoing, with interim data alongside continued CHOPIN combination follow-up (Lancet Oncology-published Phase 2 showing improved PFS with HEPZATO plus ipilimumab/nivolumab) underpinning label/indication expansion potential.

pulse: Delcath keeps executing: Q1 2026 revenue of $25M at roughly 85% gross margin, about 36% HEPZATO volume growth, $89M cash with no debt, and reiterated $100M+ 2026 guidance, while the DELUMA breast-cancer trial has now dosed its first patient, marking the move beyond uveal melanoma into a far larger market. Russell value-index inclusion, a Zacks upgrade to hold, and a reaffirmed Craig-Hallum Buy rating support the story as the stock trades near 52-week highs with a wide range of analyst price targets. Near-term focus is the early-August Q2 print as the next real test of full-year guidance, alongside continued enrollment in the breast and colorectal cancer expansion trials.
